title: AI Scope Narrative Generator — IICRC-Cited Restoration Reports in Seconds
description: See how RestoreAssist's AI-powered scope writer uses Claude to generate a complete 7-section IICRC S500-cited scope narrative from your inspection data — ready for insurance submission in under 30 seconds.
transcript: RestoreAssist's AI scope narrative generator produces a complete 7-section IICRC S500-cited scope narrative from your inspection data in under 30 seconds. The generator uses the Claude AI model with a system prompt that encodes IICRC S500:2025 terminology, AS/NZS standards, and Australian insurance claim language. Each section — loss description, scope of works, equipment justification, drying protocol, health and safety, and cost summary — is generated with specific standard citations. Output streams live to the screen and can be exported directly to PDF.
videoScript:
G'day — today I want to show you the scope narrative generator in RestoreAssist. This is the feature that saves our users the most time on every single job, and it's the one that consistently gets the most surprised reactions when people see it for the first time.

Writing a scope narrative for an insurance claim is one of those tasks that every restoration technician knows takes too long. You've completed the assessment, you've got all your readings and photos, you know exactly what needs to happen on the job — but then you have to sit down and translate all of that into a formal written document that cites the right standards, uses the right terminology, and covers every section the insurer expects to see. For an experienced estimator, that might take 30 to 45 minutes. For a technician who's less comfortable with report writing, it can take significantly longer, and the quality is often inconsistent.

RestoreAssist generates that document in under 30 seconds. Let me show you how.

Navigate to any completed inspection — you'll want to have your moisture readings entered, your damage class and category selected, and your scope items created. Then click Generate Scope Narrative. The system begins streaming the output immediately — you can watch it write the document in real time.

The narrative is structured into seven sections. The first is the loss description — what happened, when, and what the source was. This pulls from your inspection header fields.

The second section is the assessment findings — this summarises your moisture readings, the extent of affected areas, and the materials impacted. It references the specific IICRC S500 sections that govern assessment methodology.

Third is the scope of works — a detailed, line-by-line description of the remediation activities required. Each activity is tied to an IICRC S500 citation explaining why that activity is required at this damage class and category.

Fourth is the equipment justification — this references your equipment calculator output, explaining the rationale for each piece of equipment placed, cited against the S500 ratios we covered in the equipment calculator video.

Fifth is the drying protocol — the target dry standard, the IICRC equilibrium moisture content values for each affected material, and the monitoring schedule.

Sixth is the health and safety section — this covers PPE requirements, decontamination procedures, and any AS/NZS compliance notes, particularly relevant for Category 2 and 3 jobs.

And the seventh section is the cost summary — a high-level breakdown of labour, materials, and equipment hire, formatted for insurance submission.

The whole thing is generated using the Claude AI model, with a system prompt that encodes IICRC S500:2025 terminology, Australian insurance claim language, and AS/NZS standard references. We use prompt caching on the system prompt, which means repeat calls are significantly faster and more cost-efficient.

Once the narrative streams to screen, you can edit any section inline — the output is a starting point, not a locked document. When you're happy with it, export to PDF with a single click. The PDF includes your company header, the job details, all seven sections, and a certification block for the attending technician.

The scope narrative generator is available on all paid plans. If you're tired of spending 45 minutes on report writing for every job, head to restoreassist.com.au and give it a go. There's a 14-day free trial, and you can test it on a real job today. I'll see you next time.
